name: agents-system
description: npm-shipped default plugins for agents-cli — the lightweight, no-paid-key workflows that ship with the system layer. Heavier or key-gated plugins live in .agents-extras instead.

plugins.name: code
plugins.description: Coding-workflow plugin for the manager/router engineering loop. `/code:loop` drains a queue end-to-end (single ticket or many: plan, code, test, review, rebase, fix CI, merge), `/code:dispatch` triages a single task and picks the delivery path, `/code:verify` runs the end-to-end gate (canonical test per changed surface, read from the project's CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md), `/code:review` reviews every PR opened in parallel — plus a security pass on risk-touching diffs — and merges per verdict, `/code:sprint` runs a time-boxed multi-track parallel push via `agents teams`, `/code:quality` runs a read-only code-health diagnostic and opens an HTML report, `/commit` splits the working tree into the maximum number of small logical commits.

plugins.name: git
plugins.description: git — pure git plumbing commands that aren't tied to code logic. `/git:prune` removes merged branches and worktrees with hard data-loss guards (skips anything with uncommitted changes, stashes, unmerged commits, locks, or detached HEAD), using `git rev-list --count origin/$MAIN..HEAD == 0` as the load-bearing 'nothing to lose' check. `/git:tag-release` creates and pushes an annotated release tag (never force, never re-points an existing tag). The code-aware loop (`/commit`, `/review`, `/sprint`) lives in the `code` plugin.
